expect: FM-18 upgraded from INADEQUATE (1 rule, 0 auto) to MINIMAL (2+ layers, 1+ automated). Swiss Cheese criterion requires >=2 independent automated layers for ADEQUATE.
actual: FM-18 upgraded INADEQUATE -> MINIMAL. Defense layers: 3 total (1 rule + 2 automated). Layer 1: L-903 rule (check git log before writing lesson). Layer 2: lesson_collision_check.py --staged in check.sh pre-commit (detects title-filename mismatch + staged slot conflict vs HEAD). Layer 3: claim.py next-lesson (slot reservation with conflict detection, prevents concurrent allocation of same L-NNN). Two automated layers present but not fully independent: claim.py is advisory (not enforced), check.sh is enforced. Swiss Cheese: 1 enforced auto + 1 advisory auto + 1 rule = MINIMAL, not ADEQUATE.
diff: Expected MINIMAL — got MINIMAL. For ADEQUATE: need 2 enforced automated layers. claim.py next-lesson exists but is advisory (sessions can bypass by not calling it). check.sh collision guard is enforced. To reach ADEQUATE: wire claim.py next-lesson into open_lane.py or lesson-creation tooling as mandatory step.

layer_1.mechanism: L-903: check git log --oneline before writing new lesson to detect concurrent work

layer_2.mechanism: lesson_collision_check.py --staged in check.sh (lines 102-119). Detects: (a) title-filename mismatch (L-NNN.md title says L-MMM), (b) staged new-file for slot already in HEAD
layer_2.tested: PASS — ran --staged, returned exit 0, 0 issues on clean state

layer_3.mechanism: claim.py next-lesson --session SXXX. Scans existing lessons + active claims, allocates max+1. Detects concurrent slot claims and bumps to next available.
layer_3.enforcement: advisory (caller must invoke; no structural enforcement at lesson-creation time)
layer_3.tested: PASS — allocated L-925 correctly, detected no conflict

swiss_cheese_assessment.result: MINIMAL — 1 enforced auto (check.sh) + 1 advisory auto (claim.py) + 1 rule. Advisory layer does not meet Swiss Cheese independence criterion for ADEQUATE.
swiss_cheese_assessment.upgrade_path: Wire claim.py next-lesson as mandatory step in lesson-creation flow (e.g., open_lane.py, or a lesson_create.py wrapper). This would make both automated layers enforced -> ADEQUATE.

fm18_row.name: Concurrent lesson number collision
fm18_row.change: UPGRADED INADEQUATE->MINIMAL (S410->S415). Added lesson_collision_check.py --staged in check.sh (enforced pre-commit, L-922) + claim.py next-lesson slot reservation (advisory, F-CON2). Was: 0 auto, 1 rule. Now: 2 auto (1 enforced, 1 advisory), 1 rule.
